source: Official patch notes and Path of Exile Wikis

Definition.text: Suppression is a mitigation type associated with dexterity that gives a chance to lower damage from spell hits and spell-inflicted ailments.

Purpose.text: Reduce burst severity from spell damage and related elemental ailment secondary damage by preventing a fixed portion of damage on a successful suppression roll.

Core Rules.text: By default, Suppression can be obtained only from explicit sources such as passive skills, evasion gear, and keystones; characters have no base chance. It has a per-spell-hit chance and stacks up to 100%, with 40% base suppression damage prevention per successful suppress event unless modified.

Internal Rules.text: Suppression rolls are checked for each spell hit independently; successful suppression applies only to that spell hit and any secondary damage tied to it.

Calculation Order.text: Mitigation is applied after resistances and damage reduction, then before stun and block.

Priority Rules.text: Specific suppression modifiers (for example Prevent #% of Suppressed Spell Damage) modify the default 40% prevented amount; if both apply, specific modifiers take precedence over generic suppression behavior.

Preconditions.text: Source action must be a spell Hit; source effect may be in form of passive skill, item modifier, keystone, or explicit stat granting chance to suppress spell damage.

Postconditions.text: When suppression succeeds, suppressed spell damage is reduced for that hit and secondary spell-driven ailment DPS follows the same multiplier.

Limitations.text: Suppression is independent of evasion mechanics and does not grant full immunity; it is a chance-based gate tied to each hit event.

Caps.text: Chance values can be stacked to 100%.

Formula.text: per_hit_suppressed_damage = spell_hit_damage * (base_suppressed_amount or modified_suppressed_amount); base_suppressed_amount = 40%; effective_suppressed_amount may be increased via explicit Prevent modifiers.

Conflicts.text: Suppression is not the same as dodge/evasion; base character chance is zero unless sourced.

Exceptions.text: Secondary spell damage is mitigated by suppression with same multiplier profile.

Hidden Rules.text: Patch updates rebalanced which item sources grant chance to suppress and conversion from dodge wording to suppression wording.

Developer Notes.text: Legacy dodge-to-suppression transitions for specific sources occurred in patch 3.16.0b (e.g., Blurred Form change).

Historical Changes.text: Source availability and numerical values for Suppression were changed in 3.16.0 and 3.16.0b, including movement of multiple item sources from dodge language toward suppression language.

text.note: Scourge patch family expanded sources granting chance to suppress spell damage across passives/items and converted multiple dodge-stat sources to suppression-oriented forms.
Blurred Form explicitly changed to +20% chance to suppress spell damage.

text: A 25% chance to suppress with no extra Prevent value blocks 40% of damage on 25% of spell hits.
A modifier increasing prevented suppressed damage raises the effective mitigation for each successful suppressed hit.
Suppression is not guaranteed until capped by stacking to 100%
Suppression chance and suppressed damage percentage are separate: chance to suppress is separate from prevented-amount multipliers
Suppression is a spell-only mitigation type
